title: landolphia ferruginea
intro: Landolphia ferruginea, also known as the Buxus of Africa, is a medicinal plant native to the tropical regions of West and Central Africa. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of compounds such as alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenes, which have been shown to have anti-diarrheal, anti-spasmodic, and anti-ulcerative effects, making it beneficial for treating digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions. Landolphia ferruginea is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for herbal remedies, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine.
